a_image.alt: Blueprint style mood board — Technical Drawing · Architectural

sections.a_eyebrow: Outline · Dark Mode · Technical · Monospaced · Professional
sections.a_description: White lines on deep engineering blue — the unmistakable visual language of architectural blueprints and technical drawings, translated to the screen. Every element is rendered as if drafted with a T-square and compass: precise grid lines, uppercase monospaced type, and the measured cadence of technical notation where every mark means something.

Blueprint styling appears on architecture firm portfolios, engineering company sites, and construction industry platforms. You'll find it on CAD software landing pages, technical documentation, hardware startup sites, and any project that wants to communicate precision, expertise, and the serious craft of building things.

Architectural blueprint — white technical drawings on deep engineering blue. Background: rich blueprint blue (#003366). The .inner panel has no solid background — just a subtle grid pattern via repeating-linear-gradient: thin white lines (#ffffff at 8% opacity) every 24px both horizontally and vertically, creating the classic graph-paper grid. Text is all white or near-white (#e0eaf0) with a slight blue tint. The heading is rendered as if hand-drafted — all uppercase, wide letter-spacing, with a thin underline. A pseudo-element adds a title block rectangle in the bottom-right corner (a bordered box, like the information block on a real blueprint). Zero border-radius — blueprints are precise, angular, technical. Set in JetBrains Mono — a monospace font designed for developers whose clean, technical character set perfectly captures the measured precision of architectural notation. Heading at 26px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, line-height 1.2, white. Eyebrow at 11px, uppercase, 4px letter-spacing, color rgba(255,255,255,0.5). Body at 14px, line-height 1.7, color rgba(224,234,240,0.8). Buttons: outlined rectangles with no fill — primary has white border and text, secondary has a thinner border at lower opacity. Hover: fill with semi-transparent white. Alternatives: IBM Plex Mono (wider strokes), Fira Code (ligatures), Source Code Pro (neutral mono).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
